tasks.name: implement-force-stop-infra
tasks.task:
Read plans/pipeline-force-stop.md for the full design and ALL reuse notes (R2, R3, R4) before writing any code. The foundation layer (errors/ and types/) must already be committed.

Implement the infrastructure layer change for the pipeline-force-stop feature:

Modify src/infrastructures/claudeCode.ts (File 12 in the plan):
- Add `signal?: AbortSignal` parameter to runClaude()
- Define onAbort = (): void => { if (!child.killed) child.kill('SIGTERM') }
- Register: signal?.addEventListener('abort', onAbort, { once: true })
- In the finally block: call signal?.removeEventListener('abort', onAbort)
  DO NOT add a redundant child.kill() — the existing finally guard already covers it (R2)
- After the try/finally that yields stdout, add: if (signal?.aborted) return
  This skips the non-zero exit check when the kill was intentional (R3)
- Only reach the closePromise await and RawExitError throw when signal is not aborted

Key gotchas:
- R2: child.killed is true after SIGTERM, the existing finally guard skips the redundant kill
- R3: closePromise resolves (not rejects) when child closes, so early return is safe
- R4: classifyExitError is never reached on the abort path (runClaude returns, not throws)

Run ts_checker after all changes.

tasks.name: review-force-stop-infra
tasks.task:
target_path: src/infrastructures
ng_output_path: .claude/tmp/review-force-stop-infra

Review src/infrastructures/claudeCode.ts for the pipeline-force-stop infra changes:
1. runClaude() has new optional signal?: AbortSignal parameter
2. Abort listener calls child.kill('SIGTERM') with !child.killed guard
3. addEventListener uses { once: true }
4. removeEventListener is called in finally
5. No extra child.kill() added to finally (redundant — existing guard handles it)
6. Early return `if (signal?.aborted) return` placed AFTER the try/finally block, BEFORE the closePromise await
7. No layer violations: infrastructures/ must not import from repositories/, domains/, or services/

tasks.command: npm run lint:fix && npm run build && npm run test:unit

tasks.name: commit-force-stop-infra
tasks.task:
Infrastructure layer changes are complete and passing. Commit src/infrastructures/claudeCode.ts with an appropriate conventional commit message.

Suggested message: feat(infra): add AbortSignal support to runClaude — kill child on abort
